en: Man, where do I start with this one? Because I have some stuff to say, and you all wanted to hear it.
ko: 야, 이건 어디서부터 시작해야 하지? 할 말이 좀 있는데, 너희가 다 듣고 싶어했잖아.

en: I saw the love from the last video.
Anyways, was it just me, or were some of your closest friends made during lunchtime?
ko: 어쨌든, 나만 그런 건지, 가장 친한 친구들이 점심시간에 사귄 친구들 아니야?

en: Because that was the time to talk, mess around, and not deal with any schoolwork.
ko: 왜냐면 그때가 떠들고, 장난치고, 공부 안 해도 되는 시간이었거든.

en: You were actually happy.
"What's your favorite subject, Jorge?"
"Lunch."
So many things would happen during these 30 minutes.
ko: 이 30분 동안 정말 많은 일이 일어났어.

en: During middle school, I would sit with my buddies Juan, Alexis, Harold, and several others at the school cafeteria.
ko: 중학교 때 학교 식당에서 친구들 Juan, Alexis, Harold이랑 다른 애들이랑 앉았어.

en: And we would talk about the dumbest things.
"Bro, do you brush your teeth before or after eating breakfast?"
ko: "야, 아침 먹기 전에 이 닦아, 후에 닦아?"

en: "Wait, before? That's the first thing I do in the morning."
ko: "잠깐, 전에? 아침에 제일 먼저 하는 거야."

en: "But you ruin your breakfast with all that minty breath."
"Don't tell me you brush your teeth after."
"Yeah."
We would start some dumb arguments.
Our specialty, though, was yo mama jokes.
"Man, yo mama so fat, I ran out of gas trying to drive around her."
ko: "야, 네 엄마 너무 뚱뚱해서 차로 돌아가다가 기름 떨어졌어."

en: "Well, yo mama so ugly, she's the reason why your dad left."
ko: "네 엄마 너무 못생겨서 아빠가 떠난 거야."

en: You're right. Too far.
On a lighter note, I would throw off my friends with certain magic tricks that I knew back then as well.
ko: 좀 가벼운 얘기로 넘어가면, 나는 그때 알았던 마술로 친구들을 놀라게 했어.

en: The most popular one was this quarter trick that I knew.
ko: 제일 인기 있었던 건 동전 마술이었어.

en: I already shared it before, but let me show you it again.
ko: 전에 한 번 보여줬었는데, 다시 한번 보여줄게.

en: All right, guys, welcome back to Horchata Magic!
ko: 좋아, 여러분, Horchata Magic에 다시 오신 걸 환영합니다!

en: When you guys see this quarter, I will make it move from one hand to the other.
ko: 이 동전을 한 손에서 다른 손으로 옮겨볼게요.

en: Here we go.
Three, two, one.
Ooh, you guys saw that?
One more time, one more time, one more time. Three, two, one.
ko: 한 번만 더, 한 번만 더. 셋, 둘, 하나.

en: Oh, let's do it really fast. Do it really fast. Three, two, one.
ko: 진짜 빠르게 해보자. 셋, 둘, 하나.

en: Oh, dang it! One more time, one more time, one more time. Ignore that, ignore that. Three, two, one.
ko: 아, 젠장! 한 번만 더. 무시해, 무시해. 셋, 둘, 하나.

en: All right, back to the story time.
"Bro, how did you do that?"
"I know how he did it. It's not that hard."
ko: "나 걔가 어떻게 한 건지 알아. 별로 안 어려워."

en: Someone always had to say something.
And is it really a cafeteria story time without talking about the food?
ko: 그리고 급식 얘기 없이 식당 이야기가 되겠어?

en: Because there was such a big spectrum on the food reviews.
There are mainly three different kinds of people when it came to school food.
ko: 학교 급식에 대해서 크게 세 종류의 사람이 있었어.

en: The first were the ones who absolutely hated it.
Wouldn't touch it, wouldn't eat it.
They would bring their own food.
"Ugh, that bread tastes like cardboard."
Picky eaters, am I right?
The second were the ones who didn't mind it.
ko: 두 번째는 별로 상관 안 하는 애들.

en: They would eat it and not complain.
"Eh, free food."
And the third were the ones who loved it.
They loved it a little too much, though, because they would devour the food.
ko: 근데 좀 과하게 좋아했어, 음식을 게걸스럽게 먹었거든.

en: "Hey, you gonna eat that chicken patty?"
My guy, I know the food isn't horrible, but you're different.
ko: 야, 급식이 끔찍한 건 아닌데 너는 좀 다르다.

en: Yeah, the American public school system really gave us chocolate milk and burgers to eat at 11 in the morning.
ko: 응, 미국 공립학교가 진짜 아침 11시에 초코 우유랑 버거를 줬어.

en: Let me know if your school did or does the same thing.
Every Friday, however, the school would give us pizza.
ko: 하지만 매주 금요일에는 학교에서 피자를 줬어.

en: It was a tradition.
And my buddies would bring takis, hot fries, really any chip that was spicy.
ko: 그리고 친구들은 타키, 핫프라이, 매운 과자면 다 가져왔어.

en: But at the same time, we would never admit that they were spicy.
ko: 하지만 동시에 맵다는 걸 절대 인정 안 했어.

en: "Bro, these hot fries aren't even spicy to me."
ko: "야, 이 핫프라이 나한테는 하나도 안 매워."

en: "Yeah, man, they're not hot."
I know one of you guys is like that, manning up and saying they're not spicy.
ko: 너희 중에도 이런 사람 있을 거야, 허세 부리면서 안 맵다고 하는 사람.

en: Whatever, you're cool.
We would then wash it down with strawberry milk.
Man, the stuff we would put in our bodies.
ko: 야, 우리 몸에 뭘 넣었던 거야.

en: Anyways, before we continue the story, I wanted to announce that I created a brand new channel, Horchata Clips.
ko: 어쨌든, 이야기를 계속하기 전에, 새로운 채널 Horchata Clips를 만들었다고 알려주고 싶어.

en: Yeah, I've been streaming a lot on Twitch, and I decided to make a channel.
ko: 응, Twitch에서 방송을 많이 하고 있는데

en: where I can post the stream highlights.
ko: 스트림 하이라이트를 올릴 채널을 만들기로 했어.

en: You know, the best parts, story times, gaming, commentary, reactions, all the good stuff.
ko: 있잖아, 베스트 장면, 스토리 타임, 게임, 해설, 리액션, 좋은 것만 모아서.

en: Even talking to you guys.
So subscribe, you don't wanna miss it.
I'm gonna put my heart and soul into this new channel.
ko: 이 새 채널에 전심전력을 다할 거야.

en: Anyways, back to the crazy stuff that would happen.
This one time, me and my buddies were chilling in the cafeteria like any normal afternoon, where out of nowhere, one of the school staff ladies just rushes to our table.
ko: 한번은 나랑 친구들이 평범한 오후처럼 식당에서 놀고 있었는데, 갑자기 학교 직원 아줌마 한 분이 우리 테이블로 달려왔어.

en: "How can you guys do something like that?
I need your name so I can write you guys up!"
"Wait, what?"
"Look at Aaron, he's crying, and he pointed at you guys!"
ko: "Aaron 좀 봐, 울고 있고 너희를 가리켰어!"

en: And none of us ever seen that kid in our lives.
ko: 우리 중 아무도 그 애를 본 적이 없었어.

en: And on my mom, we didn't do anything, because we didn't even know he existed.
ko: 진짜 맹세하는데, 우리는 아무것도 안 했어. 걔가 존재하는지도 몰랐으니까.

en: We were so confused, and it took so long for the staff lady to believe us.
ko: 우리가 너무 당황했고 직원 아줌마가 우리 말을 믿는 데 한참 걸렸어.

en: "Well, I'm gonna keep an eye on you guys."
"Let's go, Aaron."
Um, what was that?
What just happened?
I remember turning red from embarrassment, because it just felt like a fever dream.
ko: 창피해서 얼굴이 빨개졌던 거 기억나, 열꿈 같았거든.

en: Also, would it really be a school lunchtime without somebody making a beat and freestyling at the lunch table?
ko: 그리고 누군가 비트를 만들고 점심 테이블에서 프리스타일하는 사람 없이 학교 점심시간이 되겠어?

en: All right, so let's go, do your thing.
Mm.
That's it? That's all he did? That's all he got?
ko: 그게 다야? 걔가 한 게 그게 다야?

en: He lost tempo from what?
Oh, the pressure of the camera.
Hey! Hey! Lunch was lit!
Lunch was crazy, bro.
Every kid, every single kid in that frickin' lunchroom, there were 200 kids in there.
ko: 모든 애들, 그 빌어먹을 식당에 있는 모든 애들, 거기 200명이 있었어.

en: No music.
Pfft pfft pfft...
Pfft pfft pfft pfft pfft pfft pfft.
And the vice principal could not do anything about it.
ko: 그리고 교감 선생님이 어떻게 할 수가 없었어.

en: He could not do anything about it.
I got him telling story times now.
They would just be smacking on the table, bro.
ko: 걔네가 그냥 테이블을 두드리고 있었어, 진짜.

en: Oh my god.
That's so funny.
Yeah, we had nothing better to do, because there'd always be that one kid rapping over the beat, and homie would show off.
ko: 응, 할 게 없었거든. 항상 그 한 명이 있었어 비트 위에서 랩하는, 걔가 자랑했어.

en: "Ooh, we're gonna get out the hood with this one!"
Teachers would get so mad since we would be so loud.
ko: 선생님들이 엄청 화났어, 우리가 너무 시끄러워서.

en: "Everybody say such..."
And then we keep it going.
"Eggs, bacon, grits!"
"Everybody, may I have your attention, please?"
"My buddy Victor, he's single."
Again, we were so immature.
But there was only one thing that would get more attention than us, and that was the cafeteria fights.
ko: 우리보다 더 관심을 끄는 건 딱 하나, 식당 싸움이었어.

en: They wouldn't be too frequent, but when they occurred, it felt like we were watching a pay-per-view boxing match.
ko: 자주 있진 않았지만, 일어나면 유료 권투 시합 보는 것 같았어.

en: "All right, ladies and gentlemen, in this corner, standing at three foot ten, we have Jamal!"
ko: "자, 신사 숙녀 여러분, 이 코너에 키 117cm, Jamal입니다!"

en: "Hey, let's go, Jamal! Let's go!"
"And in this corner, standing at three foot nine, from homeroom 208, we have Marcus!"
"Man, why you gotta talk to my girl?"
ko: "야, 왜 내 여자한테 말 거는 거야?"

en: "I mean, is she really your girl if she's talking to me?"
ko: "내 말은, 나한테 말 걸면 진짜 네 여자야?"

en: "Ooh, fight, fight, fight, fight!"
All righty, guys, let's get ready to rumble!
And this one fight in particular, my guy Jamal threw a milk carton on Marcus, and it spilled all over him.
ko: 특히 이 싸움에서 Jamal이 Marcus한테 우유갑을 던졌는데 온몸에 쏟아졌어.

en: "Ooh, it's getting messy!"
Me and my boys would be sitting on our lunch table, trying to get a better view.
ko: 나랑 친구들은 점심 테이블에 앉아서 더 잘 보려고 했어.

en: And shout out to the cameraman as well, because they act like they're recording the fight, but in reality, their phone is aiming at the floor.
ko: 그리고 카메라맨한테도 감사, 싸움 찍는 척하는데 실제로는 폰이 바닥을 향하고 있고,

en: And the staff arrives so late to break it up, because by the time they arrive, the lunch bell rings,
ko: 직원들은 말리러 너무 늦게 와서, 올 때쯤이면 점심 종이 울리고

en: and we have to run back to class.
"Everybody, run!"
"Ah, I can't be late, I can't be late!"
Yeah, we would just leave the crime scene.
ko: 응, 우리는 그냥 범죄 현장을 떠났어.

en: Ah, those are some good times.
Cling, cling, cling, cling!
Yes, sir, again, subscribe to the brand new channel.
ko: 다시 한번 말하지만, 새 채널 구독해.

en: My downstairs neighbors are probably really pissed right now, because you can hear that, I'm pretty sure.
ko: 아래층 이웃이 지금 진짜 화났을 거야. 들렸을 거 확실해.
